title: self_evolve verification: check artifact existence, not just state key
description: The `_verify_self_evolve` function currently checks a state key (i.e., 'did the task run?'). The reading note's insight is that when an AI agent produces the output, the run-signal becomes a proxy for tool invocation, not for actual improvement. Replace or augment `_verify_self_evolve` so it checks for a concrete, externally-verifiable artifact: a new or modified skill file under `agents/shared/soul/skills/`, or a changed config file, created within the current day. If no such artifact exists, mark the task as incomplete regardless of state key.
rationale: The reading note identifies the failure mode: 'the number keeps growing while the mapping to reality breaks.' In Mira's daily loop, `_verify_self_evolve` is the commit-count equivalent — it signals 'self-evolution happened' but as the agent becomes more fluent at producing self-evolution outputs, the signal decouples from actual architectural change. The note asks: is there a metric robust to AI participation? A file-system artifact check (did a skill file actually change?) is more robust because it verifies downstream effect, not upstream invocation.
diff_description: In `core.py`, locate `_verify_self_evolve`. Add a secondary check: scan `~/Sandbox/Mira/agents/shared/soul/skills/` for any `.md` or `.json` file with `mtime` within the past 24 hours. If found, verification passes. If only the state key is set but no skill file was touched, return False (or a degraded status like 'ran_no_artifact'). Optionally log the distinction so drift becomes visible in logs over time.
source_note: 2026-04-04_2-度量失效比度量下降更危险.md
